title: 5 Bruins thoughts on a winless road trip, James Hagens, and playoff matchups
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Bruins finish winless road trip with 0-2-2 record after OT loss to Hurricanes in Raleigh.
Boston's playoff berth relies on losses by Blue Jackets, Red Wings, and Islanders on Thursday.
Morgan Geekie ends 17-game goalless streak with hat trick against Carolina Hurricanes.

title: When she was 11, Vanna Howard was in a refugee camp. Now she’s the first Cambodian American state senator in the U.S.
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Vanna Howard became the first Cambodian American state senator, representing Massachusetts' 1st Middlesex District.
Howard, a former refugee, arrived in the U.S. at age 11 after fleeing the Khmer Rouge genocide in Cambodia.
Her focus includes housing, healthcare, and supporting immigrant communities to meet her constituents' basic needs.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (April 8)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: An Abington one-family Cape Cod on 14 Doris Drive sold for $515,000, built in 1951 with 3 bedrooms, 2 baths.
A Boston high-rise condo at 1 Dalton St. sold for $14,000,000, marking one of the highest sales in the area.
A Belmont one-family ranch at 31 Greensbrook Way with 4 bedrooms, 4 baths sold for $2,138,000 on a 20,259-square-foot lot.

title: What businesses in Boston are worth waiting in line for?
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Brandy Melville on Newbury Street frequently has long lines due to its unique 'one size fits all' clothing policy.
Blank Street coffee shop at Boylston Street employs security due to consistently long lines at its popular location.
Brick Street Bagels in South Boston garners a strong following, contributing to its regular morning queue of customers.

title: Mass. woman pleads guilty to performing medical spa injections using counterfeit products
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Rebecca Fadanelli, 40, of Stoughton, pleaded guilty to using counterfeit products for cosmetic injections at two Massachusetts spas.
Fadanelli operated Skin Beaute Med Spas using counterfeit Botox and dermal fillers from China and Brazil, without a license.
She faces up to 20 years in prison and a $250,000 fine for importing counterfeit products and selling counterfeit drugs.

title: Man fatally shot by Boston police near Northeastern University campus identified
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Jacob Graves, 29, was fatally shot by Boston police during a mental health crisis call on Hemenway Street.
Graves attacked police and emergency responders with a 'sword-like weapon', resulting in multiple non-life-threatening injuries.
The incident near Northeastern University follows recent violent events, prompting student protests over communication issues.

title: Celtics’ Brad Stevens addresses ‘tiresome’ college basketball rumors
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Brad Stevens, Celtics' president of basketball operations, dismisses college coaching rumors, citing contentment with the Celtics.
Reports linked Stevens with college opportunities, including UNC, but he quickly ruled out leaving the Celtics.
Stevens, NBA Executive of the Year 2024, remains focused on leading Celtics' playoff pursuit in the Eastern Conference.

title: Man gets life sentence in 2024 killing of Boston father of 2
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Widmaer Lapaix, 29, was sentenced to life with parole eligibility in 15 years for the 2024 murder of Maceo Martin.
Lapaix was convicted of second-degree murder after a nine-day trial for shooting Martin during a dispute outside an apartment.
The incident occurred on October 8, 2024, when Martin was shot through a window after an altercation with his ex-wife.

title: Lindsay Clancy will admit to killing her children if trial is split into 2 parts, defense says
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Lindsay Clancy's defense seeks a two-phase trial, arguing she will admit to killing her children if granted.
A judge previously denied Clancy's request, saying guilt and criminal responsibility should be considered together.
Clancy is charged with strangling her three children in Duxbury in 2023 and plans to pursue an insanity defense.

title: Patriots trade Marte Mapu to Houston Texans
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots trade linebacker Marte Mapu to Houston Texans for a 2027 sixth-round pick.
Mapu was drafted by the Patriots in the 2023 NFL Draft's third round.
Trade frees up a roster spot and $1.5 million in cap space for New England.
